events.description: New public type name `OptionalPropertiesUpdateableProperties` uses the uncommon spelling “Updateable

details.body: New public type name `OptionalPropertiesUpdateableProperties` uses the uncommon spelling “Updateable”. Across other ARM Go SDK packages the established convention is “Updatable” (e.g., `SessionPoolUpdatableProperties` in armappcontainers). Consider renaming this model in the TypeSpec/spec (e.g., via a client name override) so the generated Go type becomes `OptionalPropertiesUpdatableProperties` before the stable release is finalized, to avoid locking in an inconsistent public API name.

events.description: `statusOption` is assigned but never used in both `ProcessMessageAsync` and `ProcessMessage`, which

details.body: `statusOption` is assigned but never used in both `ProcessMessageAsync` and `ProcessMessage`, which will generate an unused-variable warning (often treated as an error in SDK builds). Also, the error/no-throw check is reading `context?.ErrorOptions` instead of the parsed `statusOption`, so the tuple deconstruction is partly redundant. Use a discard for the second tuple element, or use `statusOption` in the `NoThrow` check (and rely on `Parse()` to handle `context == null`).

details.body: `Optional` is referenced here, but the only `Optional` type in this package is `Azure.ResourceManager.Redis.Optional` (generated under `src/Generated/Internal/Optional.cs`). Since this file is in the `Azure.ResourceManager.Redis.Models` namespace and doesn't `using Azure.ResourceManager.Redis;`, this will not compile. Add the missing using, or fully-qualify the call (e.g., `Azure.ResourceManager.Redis.Optional.IsCollectionDefined(...)`).

events.description: :yellow_circle: **Naming Issue**: `PublicNetworkAccess` is a very common type name used across many

details.body:
:yellow_circle: **Naming Issue**: `PublicNetworkAccess` is a very common type name used across many Azure services. It should have the `DurableTask` prefix for disambiguation, consistent with other enum/union types in this SDK.

**Suggested rename**: `DurableTaskPublicNetworkAccess`

**Fix in client.tsp**:
```
@@clientName(PublicNetworkAccess, "DurableTaskPublicNetworkAccess", "csharp");
```

:yellow_circle: **Naming Issue**: `PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties` lacks the `DurableTask` service prefix. Other properties types in this SDK are correctly prefixed (e.g., `DurableTaskSchedulerProperties`, `DurableTaskHubProperties`).

**Suggested rename**: `DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnectionProperties`

**Fix**: This type comes from the ARM common type `Azure.ResourceManager.CommonTypes.PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties` and does **not** exist as a named model in the TypeSpec definitio

:yellow_circle: **Naming Issue**: `PrivateEndpointConnectionPatch` lacks the `DurableTask` service prefix. The analogous patch type for schedulers is `DurableTaskSchedulerPatch`.

**Suggested rename**: `DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnectionPatch`

**Fix**: This type is auto-generated from the ARM `PrivateEndpointConnectionUpdate` template in `private-endpoints.tsp` and does **not** exist as a named model in the TypeSpec definition, so `@@clientName` cannot be used. Instead, use **SDK-side custom

:yellow_circle: **Naming Issue**: `PrivateEndpointConnectionResource` and `PrivateEndpointConnectionCollection` lack the `DurableTask` prefix. This is inconsistent with the data type `DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnectionData` which IS correctly prefixed.

**Suggested rename**: `DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnectionResource` / `DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnectionCollection`

**Fix in client.tsp**:
```
@@clientName(PrivateEndpointConnection, "DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnection", "csharp");
```

events.description: In the worker crash handler, `outDir` is populated with `msg.target.tsconfig` (a tsconfig path), not

details.body:
In the worker crash handler, `outDir` is populated with `msg.target.tsconfig` (a tsconfig path), not the target output directory. This will mislead downstream reporting/diagnostics and can break code that assumes `outDir` is a real output directory. Use the parsed target’s `outDir` if available (e.g., keep `parsed` in scope), or fall back to a clearly “unknown” value like an empty string or `msg.packageRoot`.
```suggestion
          outDir: msg.packageRoot ?? "",
```

details.body: Defaulting the shim `type` to `"module"` when `target.moduleType` is absent can silently produce an invalid output package.json for `condition: require` targets that weren’t migrated (Node will treat `.js` as ESM and `require()` will fail). Since this PR moves to explicit `moduleType`, consider making this failure explicit by validating config (e.g., require `moduleType: commonjs` when `condition` is `"require"`), or preserve backward compatibility by inferring a safe default for `"require"` tar

The previous esbuild-based CJS emission path set `platform: "node"` for CommonJS to help Node’s CJS analysis (notably `cjs-module-lexer` named export detection). The new ESM→CJS transform omits `platform: "node"`, which can reduce interoperability/analysis quality for consumers/tools. Add `platform: "node"` here to match the prior behavior for CJS output.
```suggestion
        sourcefile: path.basename(srcPath),
        platform: "node",
```

details.body: Yeah, the diff is not great at the moment and I have a todo item to explore better alternatives. The main challenge is that to do semantic diffing, we need a semantic representation of the API, which is not provided in the api.md file.

events.description: It doesn't even need to be semantic, it just needs to do more than compare line by line. The current

details.body:
It doesn't even need to be semantic, it just needs to do more than compare line by line. The current approach is problematic when one side has a line added somewhere in the middle of the file but no differences otherwise -- with the current algorithm, every line below the added line gets marked as a difference when it really shouldn't be.

Maybe the diff calculation can be outsourced to an existing tool/library (git diff maybe somehow? I don't know)
